# Accessibility Instructions

- Build UI with semantic HTML and meaningful accessible names.
- Ensure keyboard navigation works for all interactive elements.
- Preserve visible focus indicators and logical tab order.
- Use appropriate ARIA only when native semantics are insufficient.
- Provide text alternatives for non-text content and icons that convey meaning.
- Support sufficient color contrast and do not rely on color alone to communicate state.
- Ensure dynamic content changes are announced accessibly when appropriate.
- Test interactions with keyboard-only usage and screen readers where feasible.
- Avoid inaccessible patterns such as custom clickable divs without proper semantics.
